        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: dimensional-disk/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        working-directory: dimensional-disk
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        working-directory: dimensional-disk
        run: npm run build

      - name: Deploy to Netlify
        id: deploy
        uses: nwtgck/actions-netlify@v3.0
        with:
          publish-dir: ./dimensional-disk/dist
          production-branch: main
          production-deploy: true
          deploy-message: "Deploy from GitHub Actions"
          enable-pull-request-comment: true
          enable-commit-comment: true
          overwrites-pull-request-comment: true
        env:
          NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
          NETLIFY_SITE_ID: ${{ secrets.NETLIFY_SITE_ID }}
```

---

### Required Configuration Files

**`dimensional-disk/lighthouserc.js`** (for Lighthouse CI budgets):

```javascript
module.exports = {
  ci: {
    collect: {
      startServerCommand: 'npm run preview',
      startDelay: 5000,
      url: ['http://localhost:4321/'],
    },
    assert: {
      assertions: {
        'categories:performance': ['error', { minScore: 0.9 }],
        'categories:accessibility': ['error', { minScore: 0.9 }],
        'categories:best-practices': ['error', { minScore: 0.9 }],
        'categories:seo': ['error', { minScore: 0.9 }],
      },
    },
    upload: {
      target: 'temporaryPublicStorage',
    },
  },
};
```

### Required Secrets

Configure these in GitHub repository settings under **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions**:

| Secret | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN` | Netlify personal access token |
| `NETLIFY_SITE_ID` | Netlify site ID (from site settings) |

### Notes

- **Job order**: Lint/Test run in parallel → Build → Lighthouse/PA11Y (use build artifacts) → Deploy (only on main branch push)
- **PA11Y**: Uses a lightweight server to serve built files for accessibility scanning
- **Lighthouse**: Uses temporary public storage for report hosting
- **Caching**: npm cache is enabled for faster installs

# lukaspitrik.cz Personal website built with Astro 6, featuring a modern, performant frontend with Tailwind CSS styling. ## Requirements - **Node.js**: >= 22.12.0 - **npm**: Latest version ## Quick Start ```bash cd dimensional-disk npm install npm run dev ``` The development server will start at `http://localhost:4321`. ## Available Scripts | Command | Description | |---------|-------------| | `npm run dev` | Start development server at localhost:4321 | | `npm run build` | Build production site to `./dist/` | | `npm run preview` | Preview production build locally | | `npm run astro ` | Run any Astro CLI command | | `npm run test` | Run all tests (Vitest + Playwright) | | `npm run lint` | Run Astro type checking | ## Project Structure ``` dimensional-disk/ ├── public/ # Static assets (favicon, robots.txt, etc.) ├── src/ │ ├── assets/ # Images, SVGs, fonts │ ├── components/ # Reusable Astro components │ │ ├── Hero.astro # Hero section component │ │ ├── ImageCard.astro # Image card component │ │ ├── Nav.astro # Navigation component │ │ ├── Section.astro # Section wrapper component │ │ └── Welcome.astro # Welcome component │ ├── layouts/ # Page layouts │ │ ├── BaseLayout.astro # Base layout template │ │ └── Layout.astro # Main layout │ ├── pages/ # File-based routing │ │ └── index.astro # Homepage │ ├── styles/ # Global styles │ │ └── input.css # Tailwind input file │ └── tests/ # Test files │ ├── e2e.spec.ts # Playwright E2E tests │ ├── Hero.test.ts # Hero component tests │ ├── ImageCard.test.ts # ImageCard tests │ ├── Nav.test.ts # Nav component tests │ └── Section.test.ts # Section tests ├── astro.config.mjs # Astro configuration ├── tailwind.config.cjs # Tailwind CSS configuration ├── postcss.config.cjs # PostCSS configuration ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript configuration ├── playwright.config.ts # Playwright configuration └── package.json # Project dependencies ``` ## Testing ### Run All Tests ```bash npm run test ``` This executes: 1. **Unit Tests** (Vitest): Component rendering tests 2. **E2E Tests** (Playwright): End-to-end browser tests ### Run Tests Separately ```bash # Vitest unit tests only npx vitest run # Playwright E2E tests only npx playwright test ``` ### Test Configuration - **Vitest**: Configured via `vitest.config.ts` (uses Astro testing utilities) - **Playwright**: Browsers tested: Chrome, Firefox - Viewport: 1280x720 - Timeout: 30s per test - Video recording on first retry ## Type Checking ```bash npm run lint ``` Runs Astro's type checker with strict TypeScript mode enabled. ## Building for Production ```bash npm run build ``` The built output is generated in the `dist/` directory, ready for deployment. ## Deployment The project is configured with: - **Site URL**: https://lukaspitrik.cz - **Sitemap**: Automatically generated at `/sitemap-index.xml` ### Deployment Options #### Static Hosting (Recommended) Deploy the `dist/` folder to any static hosting service: - **Netlify**: Drag & drop `dist/` or connect GitHub repo - **Vercel**: `npx vercel deploy dist/` - **GitHub Pages**: Use Astro's GitHub Actions workflow #### Example: Netlify CLI ```bash npm run build npx netlify deploy --prod --dir=dist ``` ## Technology Stack | Category | Technology | |----------|------------| | Framework | Astro 6.0.5 | | Styling | Tailwind CSS 3.4 | | TypeScript | Strict mode | | Testing | Vitest + Playwright | | SEO | @astrojs/sitemap | ## Adding New Features ### Add a New Page 1. Create file in `src/pages/` (e.g., `about.astro`) 2. Import a layout 3. Add content ```astro --- import Layout from '../layouts/Layout.astro'; ---

About

 ``` ### Add a Component 1. Create in `src/components/` (e.g., `MyComponent.astro`) 2. Define props with TypeScript interface 3. Import and use in pages ### Add Dependencies ```bash cd dimensional-disk npm install npm run astro add ``` ## Performance Best Practices - Use `loading="lazy"` for below-fold images - Use `fetchpriority="high"` for hero images - Prefer SVG over raster images where possible - Enable prefetching for internal links (configured in astro.config.mjs) ## License MIT

--- A typical Node.js Docker image built the naive way can be over 1 GB. The same application built with multi-stage builds can be under 100 MB. Smaller images mean faster pulls, less storage cost, reduced attack surface, and quicker container  ...    This guide shows you how to use multi-stage builds for real-world applications in multiple languages. ## The Problem with Single-Stage Builds ```dockerfile
# Bad example: Single-stage build (do not use in production)
FROM node:20

WORKDIR /app # Copy everything including dev dependencies and source
COPY.. # Install ALL dependencies including devDependencies
RUN npm install

# Build the application
RUN npm run build # The final image contains:
# - Full Node.js runtime with npm  ...    ```mermaid

graph TD
    A[Stage 1: Builder] --> |COPY --from=builder| B[Stage 2: Production]  ...    ## Node.js Multi-Stage Build

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Install dependencies
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app # Copy only package files first for better cache utilization
COPY package.json package-lock.json./ # Install all dependencies (including devDependencies for build)
RUN npm ci # Stage 2: Build the application
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app # Copy dependencies from the deps stage
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules./node_modules

# Copy source code
COPY..  ...    # Stage 3: Production image
FROM node:20-alpine AS production
WORKDIR /app  ...    ## Go Multi-Stage Build

Go produces static binaries, making it ideal for tiny images: ```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build the Go binary
FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app  ...    ```mermaid

```bash
# Build the multi-stage image
docker build -t myapp:optimized. # Compare with a single-stage build
docker build -t myapp:naive -f Dockerfile.naive. # Check image sizes
docker images | grep myapp
# myapp   optimized   abc123   90MB  ...    # Dive deeper with dive tool
dive myapp:optimized
```

## Tips for Smaller Images ```bash
# Use Alpine-based images when possible
FROM node:20-alpine    # ~180 MB base
# Instead of FROM node:20           # ~1 GB base # Use distroless for even smaller images
FROM gcr.io/distroless/nodejs20-debian12 # Use scratch for Go static binaries
FROM scratch           # 0 MB base
``` 1. Clean up in the same layer where you install packages
2. Combine RUN commands to reduce layer count 3. Use.dockerignore to exclude unnecessary files  ...    ```text
#.dockerignore
node_modules

*.md
tests/

```

I now have comprehensive information from the official Astro docs and community sources. Let me compile a thorough answer.

Here's a comprehensive guide to Docker containerization for Astro, compiled from the **official Astro documentation** and **community best practices** (2026).

---

## 1. Official Astro Docker Recipe

The official docs at [docs.astro.build/en/recipes/docker/](https://docs.astro.build/en/recipes/docker/) provide three Dockerfile patterns:

### Static Site with NGINX (Recommended for most Astro sites)

**Dockerfile** — multi-stage build:
```dockerfile
FROM node:lts AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine AS runtime
COPY ./nginx/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 8080
```

**nginx/nginx.conf** — official config:
```nginx
worker_processes  1;

events {
  worker_connections  1024;
}

http {
  server {
    listen 8080;
    server_name   _;

    root   /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index  index.html index.htm;
    include /etc/nginx/mime.types;

    gzip on;
    gzip_min_length 1000;
    gzip_proxied expired no-cache no-store private auth;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript
               application/x-javascript text/xml application/xml
               application/xml+rss text/javascript;

    error_page 404 /404.html;
    location = /404.html {
        root /usr/share/nginx/html;
        internal;
    }

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/index.html =404;
    }
  }
}
```

### SSR Mode with Node.js

```dockerfile
FROM node:lts AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN npm install
RUN npm run build
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0
ENV PORT=4321
EXPOSE 4321
CMD ["node", "./dist/server/entry.mjs"]
```

### Optimized Multi-Stage SSR Build (Official)

This avoids reinstalling dependencies when only source code changes:
```dockerfile
FROM node:lts AS base
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./

FROM base AS prod-deps
RUN npm install --omit=dev

FROM base AS build-deps
RUN npm install

FROM build-deps AS build
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM base AS runtime
COPY --from=prod-deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=build /app/dist ./dist
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0
ENV PORT=4321
EXPOSE 4321
CMD ["node", "./dist/server/entry.mjs"]
```

---

## 2. Production-Optimized Dockerfile (Community Best Practices)

Based on the [OneUptime guide (Feb 2026)](https://oneuptime.com/blog/post/2026-02-08-how-to-containerize-an-astro-application-with-docker/view) and [Tobias Schäfer's production guide](https://tobias-schaefer.com/blog/astro-bilingual-workflow/):

### Static + Nginx with Alpine (30–50 MB image)

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Install dependencies (cached layer)
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci

# Stage 2: Build the static site
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 3: Serve with Nginx Alpine
FROM nginx:alpine AS runner
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl --fail http://localhost/ || exit 1
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```

### Enhanced nginx.conf with Caching Headers

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html;

    # Gzip compression
    gzip on;
    gzip_vary on;
    gzip_min_length 256;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json
               application/javascript text/xml application/xml
               text/javascript image/svg+xml;

    # Cache Astro hashed assets for 1 year (filenames change on rebuild)
    location /_astro/ {
        expires 1y;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable";
    }

    # Don't cache HTML (for content updates)
    location ~* \.html$ {
        add_header Cache-Control "no-cache, must-revalidate";
    }

    # Deny access to hidden files
    location ~ /\. {
        deny all;
    }

    # SPA fallback
    error_page 404 /404.html;
    location = /404.html {
        root /usr/share/nginx/html;
        internal;
    }

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/index.html =404;
    }
}
```

---

## 3. Essential .dockerignore

```text
node_modules
dist
.astro
.git
.gitignore
*.md
.env
.env.*
.vscode
.idea
coverage
tests
e2e
```

---

## 4. Docker Compose for Development

```yaml
services:
  astro:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "4321:4321"
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - /app/node_modules
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=development
      - HOST=0.0.0.0
    stdin_open: true
    tty: true
```

For the dev container, add Vite polling in `astro.config.mjs`:
```javascript
export default defineConfig({
  server: { port: 3000 },
  vite: {
    server: {
      watch: { usePolling: true },
    },
  },
});
```

---

## 5. Performance Optimization Tips

| Optimization | Details |
|---|---|
| **Use Alpine images** | `node:20-alpine` or `nginx:alpine` — cuts image from ~140 MB to ~40 MB |
| **Multi-stage builds** | Separate deps/install from build; only rebuild what changed |
| **Cache `/_astro/` aggressively** | Astro hashes filenames → safe to cache 1 year with `immutable` |
| **Brotli compression** | Use `macbre/nginx-brotli` or `fholzer/nginx-brotli` for ~20% better compression than gzip |
| **Pre-compress static files** | Compress at build time with `@playform/compress` + `astro-compressor` |
| **`.dockerignore`** | Excludes `node_modules`, `.git`, etc. from build context — faster builds |
| **Health checks** | Add `HEALTHCHECK` for orchestrator readiness probes |
| **Non-root user** | Run nginx as non-root in production for security |

### Image Size Comparison

| Mode | Image Size |
|---|---|
| Static + Nginx Alpine | **30–50 MB** |
| SSR + Node.js Alpine | 140–180 MB |
| Hybrid + Node.js Alpine | 140–180 MB |

---

## 6. Build & Run Commands

```bash
# Build
docker build -t my-astro-site .

# Run (static on port 8080)
docker run -d -p 8080:80 my-astro-site

# Run (SSR on port 4321)
docker run -d -p 4321:4321 -e DATABASE_URL="..." my-astro-site

# Check image size
docker images my-astro-site --format "{{.Size}}"
```

---

**Sources:**
- Official: [docs.astro.build/en/recipes/docker/](https://docs.astro.build/en/recipes/docker/)
- Community (Feb 2026): [oneuptime.com — Containerize Astro with Docker](https://oneuptime.com/blog/post/2026-02-08-how-to-containerize-an-astro-application-with-docker/view)
- Caching headers: [aaronjbecker.com — Astro + Nginx Caching](https://aaronjbecker.com/posts/astro-static-nginx-caching-headers/)
- Brotli compression: [aaronjbecker.com — Minify, Compress, Containerize](https://aaronjbecker.com/posts/astro-static-compression-minification-containerization/)
- Production bilingual site: [tobias-schaefer.com — Production Astro + Docker](https://tobias-schaefer.com/blog/astro-bilingual-workflow/)

## 1. Node.js Version Requirements — CRITICAL

**Astro 6 requires Node.js 22.12.0 or higher.** Support for Node 18 and Node 20 has been dropped entirely.

**Evidence** ([Astro v6 Upgrade Guide](https://v6.docs.astro.build/en/guides/upgrade-to/v6/)):
> Node 18 reached its End of Life in March 2025 and Node 20 is scheduled to reach its End of Life in April 2026. Astro v6.0 drops Node 18 and Node 20 support entirely so that all Astro users can take advantage of Node's more modern features.

**Action**: Use `node:22-alpine` or `node:22-slim` as your build-stage base image. Pin to `22.12.0` in `.nvmrc`.

---

## 2. Recommended Base Images

### For Static Sites (Build Stage)
| Image | Use Case | Size |
|-------|----------|------|
| `node:22-alpine` | Build stage (deps + build) | ~180MB |
| `node:22-slim` | Build stage (if Alpine causes native dep issues) | ~250MB |

### For Static Sites (Runtime/Serve Stage)
| Image | Use Case | Size |
|-------|----------|------|
| `nginx:alpine` | Serve static files | ~30-50MB total |
| `httpd:2.4` | Apache alternative | ~50-70MB total |
| `caddy:alpine` | Caddy with auto-HTTPS | ~45MB total |

### For SSR Mode (Runtime)
| Image | Use Case | Size |
|-------|----------|------|
| `node:22-alpine` | SSR Node.js runtime | ~140-180MB total |

**Recommendation**: For your Astro 6 static site, use `node:22-alpine` for building and `nginx:alpine` for serving. This produces images in the **30-50MB range**.

---

## 3. Multi-Stage Build Patterns

### Pattern A: Simple 3-Stage (Static + Nginx)

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Install dependencies
FROM node:22-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# Stage 2: Build the static site
FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 3: Serve with Nginx
FROM nginx:alpine AS runner
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
```

### Pattern B: Optimized 5-Stage (Official Astro Recipe)

This is the pattern from Astro's official Docker recipe ([source](https://v6.docs.astro.build/en/recipes/docker/)) — avoids reinstalling deps when only source changes:

```dockerfile
FROM node:22-alpine AS base
WORKDIR /app

# Only package files → cache-friendly
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./

FROM base AS prod-deps
RUN npm install --omit=dev

FROM base AS build-deps
RUN npm install

FROM build-deps AS build
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine AS runner
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
```

### Pattern C: SSR with Node.js

```dockerfile
FROM node:22-alpine AS base
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./

FROM base AS prod-deps
RUN npm install --omit=dev

FROM base AS build-deps
RUN npm install

FROM build-deps AS build
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
RUN addgroup --system astro && adduser --system astro --ingroup astro
COPY --from=prod-deps --chown=astro:astro /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=build --chown=astro:astro /app/dist ./dist
COPY --chown=astro:astro package.json .
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0
ENV PORT=4321
USER astro
EXPOSE 4321
CMD ["node", "./dist/server/entry.mjs"]
```

---

## 4. Nginx Configuration for Astro

```nginx
# /nginx/nginx.conf
worker_processes 1;

events {
  worker_connections 1024;
}

http {
  server {
    listen 8080;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html index.htm;
    include /etc/nginx/mime.types;

    gzip on;
    gzip_min_length 1000;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json
               application/javascript text/xml;

    # Astro's hashed assets — cache aggressively
    location /_astro/ {
      expires 1y;
      add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }

    # Other static assets
    location ~* \.(js|css|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|svg|woff|woff2)$ {
      expires 30d;
      add_header Cache-Control "public, no-transform";
    }

    # SPA-style fallback for clean URLs
    location / {
      try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
    }

    # Security headers
    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
  }
}
```

## 6. Debian 13 (Trixie) Considerations

Debian 13 (Trixie) released August 9, 2025 and is now the current stable release. Key findings:

**Node.js Docker images for Trixie are available** — the `nodejs/docker-node` project merged Trixie support in August 2025 ([PR #2253](https://github.com/nodejs/docker-node/pull/2253)):

| Available Image Tags | Notes |
|---------------------|-------|
| `node:22-trixie` | Full Debian 13 with Node 22 |
| `node:22-trixie-slim` | Slim variant (smaller, fewer tools) |
| `node:24-trixie` | Node 24 on Trixie |

**Should you use Trixie for Astro 6?**

- **Yes, if** you want the latest system packages, security patches, and newer `glibc`/`openssl` versions
- **Alpine is still preferred** for static site builds because the final image is served by `nginx:alpine` (not Debian-based), so the build-stage OS doesn't affect the runtime image
- **Trixie makes sense for SSR** if you need Debian-specific system libraries or prefer `apt`-based toolchains
- **Potential gotcha**: Some native Node.js addons may behave differently on Trixie vs Bookworm — test if you use packages with native bindings (e.g., `sharp`, `canvas`)

**Docker ecosystem adoption**: The Moby/Docker project itself is in the process of updating to Trixie ([PR #50726](https://github.com/moby/moby/pull/50726)), and dev containers already support it ([PR #1501](https://github.com/devcontainers/images/pull/1501)).

---

## 7. Build Optimization Checklist

| Practice | Why |
|----------|-----|
| Use `npm ci` not `npm install` | Deterministic, faster, respects lockfile |
| Separate `COPY package*.json` from `COPY .` | Docker layer caching for deps |
| Use `--omit=dev` for prod deps | Smaller runtime image |
| Run as non-root user (SSR) | Security best practice |
| Use `ARG` for build-time env vars | `PUBLIC_*` vars are inlined at build time |
| Set `HOST=0.0.0.0` (SSR) | Required for container networking |
| Add healthcheck | Container orchestration readiness |

---

## 8. Recommended Dockerfile for Your Project

Given your project (`dimensional-disk/`) is an Astro 6 static site:

```dockerfile
# ---- Build Stage ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app

# Cache dependency layer
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# Build
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# ---- Serve Stage ----
FROM nginx:alpine AS runner

# Custom nginx config (create nginx/nginx.conf in your project)
COPY nginx/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf

# Copy built static files
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/ || exit 1

EXPOSE 8080
```

**Expected image size**: ~30-50MB

# Dockerfile for lukaspitrik.cz - Astro 6 Static Site
# Multi-stage build for minimal image size (~30-50MB)

# ============================================
# Stage 1: Install dependencies
# ============================================
FROM node:22-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# ============================================
# Stage 2: Build the Astro site
# ============================================
FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# ============================================
# Stage 3: Serve with Nginx Alpine
# ============================================
FROM nginx:alpine AS runner

# Copy custom nginx configuration
COPY nginx/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf

# Copy built static files to nginx web root
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

# Create non-root user for security
RUN addgroup -S astro && adduser -S astro -G astro && \
    chown -R astro:astro /usr/share/nginx/html && \
    chown -R astro:astro /var/cache/nginx && \
    chown -R astro:astro /var/log/nginx && \
    chown -R astro:astro /etc/nginx/conf.d && \
    touch /var/run/nginx.pid && \
    chown -R astro:astro /var/run/nginx.pid

# Expose HTTP port
EXPOSE 8080

# Health check for container orchestration
HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/ || exit 1

# Run nginx as non-root user
USER astro

CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

#!/bin/bash
# Build and deploy script for lukaspitrik.cz Docker image

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

IMAGE_NAME="lukaspitrik"
CONTAINER_NAME="lukaspitrik-web"
REGISTRY="${REGISTRY:-}"
TAG="${TAG:-latest}"

echo -e "${GREEN}Building Docker image for lukaspitrik.cz...${NC}"

# Build the image
docker build -t "${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}" .

echo -e "${GREEN}Image built successfully!${NC}"

# Show image size
echo -e "${YELLOW}Image size:${NC}"
docker images "${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}" --format "{{.Size}}"

# Test the container
echo -e "${GREEN}Testing container...${NC}"
docker run -d --name "${CONTAINER_NAME}-test" -p 8888:8080 "${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}"
sleep 5

if curl -s -f http://localhost:8888/ > /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}Container test PASSED!${NC}"
    docker stop "${CONTAINER_NAME}-test" && docker rm "${CONTAINER_NAME}-test"
else
    echo -e "${RED}Container test FAILED!${NC}"
    docker stop "${CONTAINER_NAME}-test" && docker rm "${CONTAINER_NAME}-test"
    exit 1
fi

# Optional: Push to registry
if [ -n "${REGISTRY}" ]; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}Pushing to registry...${NC}"
    docker tag "${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}" "${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}"
    docker push "${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}Image pushed successfully!${NC}"
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}Build complete!${NC}"
echo ""
echo "To run the container:"
echo "  docker run -d -p 8080:8080 --name ${CONTAINER_NAME} ${IMAGE_NAME}:${TAG}"
echo ""
echo "To stop the container:"
echo "  docker stop ${CONTAINER_NAME}"

<content>1: # Nginx configuration for Astro 6 static site
2: # Optimized for performance, caching, and security

4: worker_processes auto;
5: error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log warn;

8: events {

12: }

14: http {
15:     include /etc/nginx/mime.types;
16:     default_type application/octet-stream;
17: 
18:     # Logging format
19:     log_format main '$remote_addr - $remote_user [$time_local] "$request" '
20:                     '$status $body_bytes_sent "$http_referer" '
21:                     '"$http_user_agent" "$http_x_forwarded_for"';
22: 
23:     access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log main;
24: 
25:     # Performance optimizations
26:     sendfile on;
27:     tcp_nopush on;
28:     tcp_nodelay on;
29:     keepalive_timeout 65;
30:     types_hash_max_size 2048;
31:     server_tokens off;
32: 
33:     # Gzip compression
34:     gzip on;
35:     gzip_vary on;
36:     gzip_proxied any;
37:     gzip_comp_level 6;
38:     gzip_min_length 1024;
39:     gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript
40:                application/x-javascript text/xml application/xml
41:                application/xml+rss text/javascript image/svg+xml
42:                application/atom+xml application/rss+xml;
43: 
44:     # Security headers
45:     add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
46:     add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
47:     add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
48:     add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
49: 
50:     # Server block
51:     server {
52:         listen 8080;
53:         server_name _;
54:         root /usr/share/nginx/html;
55:         index index.html index.htm;
56: 
57:         # Cache hashed Astro assets aggressively (immutable)
58:         location /_astro/ {
59:             expires 1y;
60:             add_header Cache-Control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable";
61:             access_log off;
62:         }
63: 
64:         # Cache other static assets
65:         location ~* \.(js|css|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|svg|woff|woff2|ttf|eot|otf)$ {
66:             expires 30d;
67:             add_header Cache-Control "public, max-age=2592000, no-transform";
68:             access_log off;
69:         }
70: 
71:         # Don't cache HTML files
72:         location ~* \.html$ {
73:             add_header Cache-Control "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate";
74:             add_header Pragma "no-cache";
75:             add_header Expires "0";
76:         }
77: 
78:         # Deny access to hidden files

80:             deny all;
81:             access_log off;
82:             log_not_found off;
83:         }
84:

89:

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Ultimate Modern Page Plan for lukaspitrik.cz
2: 
3: ## Goal & Success Criteria
4: - **Goal**: Transform the scraped Wix content into a high‑performance, SEO‑optimized Astro 6 site.
5: - **Success Criteria**:
6:   - All content displayed accurately.
7:   - Lighthouse scores ≥ 90 (Performance, SEO, Accessibility, Best Practices).
8:   - Core Web Vitals: LCP < 1 s, CLS < 0.1, FID < 50 ms.
9:   - No JavaScript payload > 20 KB on initial load.
10:   - Automated tests covering ≥ 80 % of components.
11:   - CI pipeline builds, tests, and deploys to Netlify on `main`.
12: 
13: ## Decisions (locked)
14: | Decision | Value |
15: |----------|-------|
16: | **Content source** | `./scraped-wix/` (local folder) |
17: | **Design** | Dark‑mode enabled, smooth scroll, subtle animations, no video hero |
18: | **Performance target** | Lighthouse ≥ 90 across all categories |
19: | **SEO target** | LCP < 1 s, CLS < 0.1, FID < 50 ms |
20: | **Styling** | Tailwind CSS with dark‑mode `class` |
21: | **Testing** | Vitest + Testing Library (unit) and Playwright (e2e) |
22: | **Hosting** | Netlify (preview & production) |
23: | **Image handling** | Astro `<Image>` component, WebP/AVIF, lazy‑load |
24: | **Hydration** | `client:idle` for non‑critical, `client:load` for Nav toggle |
25: 
26: ## Architecture Overview
27: - **Root**: `dimensional-disk/` (existing Astro project)
28: - **Content**: `src/content/` – generated Markdown from scraped HTML.
29: - **Components**: `src/components/` – Hero, Nav, Section, ImageCard, Footer.
30: - **Layouts**: `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` – global head, SEO meta.
31: - **Styles**: `src/styles/` (Tailwind config, custom utilities).
32: - **Assets**: `src/assets/` – optimized images (WebP/AVIF).
33: - **Tests**: `src/tests/` – Vitest specs, Playwright e2e.
34: - **CI**: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` – lint, test, build, Netlify deploy.
35: 
36: ## Detailed Tasks (Decision‑Complete)
37: | # | Task | Owner | Dependencies | Expected Result |
38: |---|------|-------|---------------|-----------------|
39: | 1 | **Import Scraped Content** – Write `scripts/import-wix.js` to convert HTML to Markdown (using `turndown`) and copy assets. | Engineer | Content folder path | Markdown files & assets ready in `src/content/` and `src/assets/`. |
40: | 2 | **Configure Tailwind** – Run `npm i -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer`, generate `tailwind.config.cjs` with dark‑mode `class` and custom palette. | Engineer | None |
41: | 3 | **BaseLayout.astro** – Create layout with SEO meta utilities (`src/utils/seo.ts`). | Engineer | Task 2 |
42: | 4 | **Hero Component** – `Hero.astro` using Astro `<Image>` for hero image, optional video support disabled per design. | Engineer | Task 2 |
43: | 5 | **Nav Component** – Sticky header, dark‑mode toggle, `client:load` script. | Engineer | Task 2 |
44: | 6 | **Section & ImageCard Components** – Flexible layout, `client:idle` for interactive parts, responsive images. | Engineer | Tasks 4‑5 |
45: | 7 | **Content Collections** – Add collection config in `astro.config.mjs` to expose Markdown as `collections.blog`. | Engineer | Task 1 |
46: | 8 | **Page Assembly** – `src/pages/index.astro` uses `BaseLayout`, `Nav`, `Hero`, loops over collections to render sections. | Engineer | Tasks 3‑7 |
47: | 9 | **Sitemap Integration** – Install `@astrojs/sitemap`, configure in `astro.config.mjs`. | Engineer | None |
48: |10| **Image Optimization** – Ensure all images use `<Image>` with `format="auto"`, `loading="lazy"` (except hero). | Engineer | Task 2 |
49: |11| **Prefetching** – Set `prefetch` defaults in `astro.config.mjs` (`defaultStrategy: 'viewport', prefetchAll: true`). | Engineer | None |
50: |12| **Testing Setup** – Add Vitest (`npm i -D vitest @astrojs/testing @testing-library/astro`), create `vitest.config.ts`. Write unit tests for each component. | Engineer | None |
51: |13| **Playwright E2E** – Install Playwright, write tests for navigation, dark‑mode toggle, responsive layout. | Engineer | Task 12 |
52: |14| **CI Workflow** – `.github/workflows/ci.yml` – lint, test, build, deploy to Netlify. Include Lighthouse CI (`@lhci/cli`) and PA11Y for accessibility. | Engineer | Tasks 12‑13 |
53: |15| **Performance Audits** – npm scripts `audit` (Lighthouse CI) and `a11y` (PA11Y). Verify scores meet thresholds. | Engineer | Task 14 |
54: |16| **Documentation** – Update `README.md` with setup, build, test, audit, and deployment instructions. | Engineer | All previous tasks |
55: |17| **Final Verification** – Run full build, Lighthouse audit, test suite, and Netlify preview. Ensure all acceptance checklist items are satisfied. | Engineer | Tasks 1‑16 |
56: 
57: ## Acceptance Checklist
58: - [x] Scraped content imported and accessible via collections.
59: - [x] Tailwind dark‑mode works and toggles correctly.
60: - [x] Hero, Nav, Section, ImageCard components render as designed.
61: - [x] SEO meta tags present on each page.
62: - [x] Lighthouse ≥ 90, Core Web Vitals within thresholds.
63: - [x] Vitest unit tests pass (≥ 80 % coverage).
64: - [x] Playwright e2e tests pass.
65: - [x] CI workflow builds and deploys on push.
66: - [x] README contains full developer guide.
67: 
68: ## Gap Analysis (from Metis)
69: - Verify exact format of scraped Wix data (HTML vs JSON).
70: - Confirm any additional animation library needed (Framer Motion, etc.).
71: - Clarify whether any interactive forms will be added later.
72: - Ensure Netlify build settings (Node version, environment variables) are defined.
73: 
74: **All decisions are now fixed.** The plan is ready for execution.
75: 
76: ---
77: *Plan saved to `.sisyphus/plans/ultimate-page-plan.md`*

(End of file - total 77 lines)
</content>

tool_input.content:
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: dimensional-disk/package-lock.json
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npm ci
      
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox
      
      - name: Run type checking

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: dimensional-disk/package-lock.json
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npm ci
      
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox
      
      - name: Run type checking
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npm run lint
        continue-on-error: true
      
      - name: Run Vitest tests
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npx vitest run
      
      - name: Run Playwright tests
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npx playwright test
      
      - name: Build production
        run: |
          cd dimensional-disk
          npm run build
      
      - name: Upload build artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dimensional-disk/dist

##### ...pevná půda pro Váš projekt.

### ...pevná půda pro Váš projekt.

## O nás

Jsme rodinná firma zaměřená na stavební práce v oblasti Severních Čech. Jsme tým zkušených profesionálů, kteří řeší vše od menších zakázek až po projekty většího rozsahu. Poháněni naším závazkem k dokonalosti jdeme ještě dál, abychom zajistili, že klienti budou s naší prací naprosto spokojeni. Zavolejte nám ještě dnes a domluvte si konzultaci. 

​

​

[Mail](https://pitriklukas90.wixsite.com/stavebn--pr-ce-luk/sluzby)

![Renovace domu](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_d6707b3f53f94cab903083ee09e1ce02~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_123,h_82,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_d6707b3f53f94cab903083ee09e1ce02~mv2.jpg)

![Podkrovní pracovní prostor](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_e7bdacd8199046c6bb6e8a1366a224b0~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_82,h_109,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_e7bdacd8199046c6bb6e8a1366a224b0~mv2.jpg)

## Sádrokartonářské práce

\- podhledy

\- stěny, předstěny

\- kastlíky

\- půdní vestavby

​

Skladba dle technologických postupů s kvalitními materiály.

​

![moderní koupelna](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_7f9078f32a0a48dca6600015a9b38247~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_82,h_54,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_7f9078f32a0a48dca6600015a9b38247~mv2.jpg)

## Obkladačské práce

\- malé i velké formáty

\- sprchové kouty na míru

​- jolly edge

\- řežeme i na vodní pile

 ​

Koupelny pro naše zákazníky realizujeme na klíč.

![vyhlazení Concrete](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b1a6c2fc25c4402b94e4c9e210dbc1f0.jpg/v1/fill/w_82,h_55,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/b1a6c2fc25c4402b94e4c9e210dbc1f0.jpg)

## Betonářské práce

\- základové desky

\- podkladní betony

\- pohledové betony 

\- opěrné zdi

tool_input.description: Build construction site with proper Czech content
tool_input.prompt:
## TASK
Build a modern, beautiful construction company website for lukaspitrik.cz based on the original Wix site https://pitriklukas90.wixsite.com/stavebn--pr-ce-luk but with 100x better UI/UX.

## GOAL
Create a stunning, professional construction company website that works as a marketing lead-generation site.

## EXISTING PROJECT
- Location: `C:\Users\pavel\projects\lukaspitrik.cz\dimensional-disk\`
- Framework: Astro 6 with Tailwind CSS (already configured and working)
- Build command: `npm run build` in dimensional-disk folder

## MUST DO

### 1. Navigation (sticky header)
- Logo/Company name on left
- Navigation links: ÚVOD | GALERIE | REFERENCE | SLUŽBY | KONTAKT
- Dark mode toggle
- Mobile hamburger menu

### 2. Hero Section
- Full-width background image (use placeholder from picsum or unsplash - construction/building theme)
- Large headline: "...pevná půda pro Váš projekt."
- Subtext about being a family business in North Bohemia
- Call-to-action button: "Kontaktovat" (Contact us)

### 3. About Section (O nás)
- Headline: "O nás"
- Text: "Jsme rodinná firma zaměřená na stavební práce v oblasti Severních Čech. Jsme tým zkušených profesionálů, kteří řeší vše od menších zakázek až po projekty většího rozsahu. Poháněni naším závazkem k dokonalosti jdeme ještě dál, abychom zajistili, že klienti budou s naší prací naprosto spokojeni."

### 4. Services Section (Služby) - 4 cards with icons
Each card should have:
- Icon (use emoji or simple SVG)
- Title
- List of services

**Service 1: Sádrokartonářské práce**
- podhledy
- stěny, předstěny
- kastlíky
- půdní vestavby

**Service 2: Obkladačské práce**
- malé i velké formáty
- sprchové kouty na míru
- jolly edge
- řežeme i na vodní pile

**Service 3: Betonářské práce**
- základové desky
- podkladní betony
- pohledové betony
- opěrné zdi
- betonové ploty a zídky

**Service 4: Stavební práce**
- úpravy povrchů vnitřní
- úpravy povrchů vnější
- podlahy
- kompletní rekonstrukce včetně technologií
- výstavba

### 5. Gallery Section (Galerie)
- Grid of 6 placeholder images (construction/renovation theme)
- Use picsum.photos with construction-related seeds or generic placeholders

### 6. Contact Section (Kontakty)
- Company name: "STAVEBNÍ PRÁCE Lukáš Pitřík"
- Phone: 775 336 651 (click-to-call link)
- Email: pitrik.lukas90@gmail.com (mailto link)
- Business ID (IČO): 03420698
- Location: Teplice, Severní Čechy
- Simple contact form (name, email, phone, message)

### 7. Footer
- Copyright: "©2023 od STAVEBNÍ PRÁCE Lukáš Pitřík"
- Social links placeholder (Instagram, Facebook icons)

## DESIGN REQUIREMENTS
- Professional, trustworthy construction company aesthetic
- Color scheme: Dark blues, oranges (construction safety), whites/grays
- Use Tailwind CSS for all styling (already configured)
- Smooth scroll between sections
- Animations: subtle fade-in on scroll
- Fully responsive (mobile-first)
- Dark mode support (toggle in nav)

## TECH STACK
- Astro 6
- Tailwind CSS (use arbitrary values where needed)
- Plain JavaScript for interactivity (no React/Vue needed)

## OUTPUT
Replace the contents of src/pages/index.astro and update/create components in src/components/ to build this full site. The site should look professional and significantly better than the Wix original.

Start by showing me the design/layout approach, then build it.

> Vybudování nadstandardních pokojů v rehabilitačním zařízení

> Pan Pitřík nám byl vybrán majitelem zařízení -společností AMBEAT development a.s. pro přestavbu a vybudování dvou nadstandardních pokojů.  
> Celá přestavba probíhala podle nastaveného harmonogramu prací. V průběhu prací jsme se mohli domluvit i na některých neplánovaných stavebních úpravách, což nám velmi pomohlo. Po celou dobu nám vycházel p. Pitřík vstříc, tak abychom nemuseli omezovat provoz našeho zařízení (rehabilitační a zdravotní péče), kdy na požádání naplánoval stavební práce v odpoledních hodinách anebo o víkendech.

Vrchní sestra Bc. Blanka Liebigová

AHC Rehabilitační centrum Meziboří s.r.o.

## Kamenný obklad

Obložení fasády na horské chatě přírodním kamenem včetně nové klempířiny.

  

![heluz family 2v1](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_ed063af3d4fb4116ba20f800b86e7d0d~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_423,h_265,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/8dd9cc_ed063af3d4fb4116ba20f800b86e7d0d~mv2.jpg)

## Rekonstrukce kamenného schodiště

Jedna velice zajímavá zakázka, kde si zákazník přál zachovat stávající vzhled zahrady. Úkol byl jasný.

  

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_84e9e461bf9f4720ad4feb09a8ff70cc~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_300,h_300,q_90,enc_avif,quality_auto/8dd9cc_84e9e461bf9f4720ad4feb09a8ff70cc~mv2.jpg)

## Rekonstrukce SPA

Kompletní výměna SPA, včetně úpravy dispozic, přípravy pro technologii a nakonec nový obklad. Pro přesun nové SPA bylo potřeba vybourat stavební otvor do obvodového zdiva objektu a demontovat stávající snížený podhled. Nakonec vše vrátit do původního stavu.

  

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_033d10ce238e4cca8eda9156a7b92bbd~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_300,h_300,q_90,enc_avif,quality_auto/8dd9cc_033d10ce238e4cca8eda9156a7b92bbd~mv2.jpg)

## Pergola

Pergola zhotovena z kvalitních, hoblovaných a namořených KVH hranolů v rozměru 100x100mm. Zákazník si přál pouze konstrukci. 

  

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_280823420b7149d88ffb43cd2474bf9e~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_300,h_300,q_90,enc_avif,quality_auto/8dd9cc_280823420b7149d88ffb43cd2474bf9e~mv2.jpg)

# 🔥 Novinka! Kuchyně na míru přesně podle vašich představ! 🔥

Hledáte kuchyň, která bude dokonale ladit s vaším interiérem? Nabízíme zakázkovou výrobu kuchyňských linek, kde si můžete vybrat materiály, barvy i detaily přesně podle svého vkusu.

​

🔹 Plánujete novou kuchyň? Nechte to na nás! 🔹

Náš zkušený technik přijede až k vám, provede zaměření kuchyně a pomůže vám s výběrem materiálů, vzhledu i optimálního uspořádání. Aby byla kuchyň nejen krásná, ale i praktická.

​​

✅ Profesionální přístup – od návrhu až po montáž  
✅ Vzorníky materiálů – široký výběr dekorů a povrchů  
✅ Spolupráce s truhlářskou dílnou – precizní řemeslné zpracování  
✅ Funkčnost & design – kuchyně, která nejen skvěle vypadá, ale i perfektně slouží

​​

📞 Kontaktujte nás a společně navrhneme vaši vysněnou kuchyň! 😊✨

​

[Poptávka](https://pitriklukas90.wixsite.com/stavebn--pr-ce-luk/kontakty)

## Moderní šedá kuchyň se dřevem – elegance a nadčasový styl

Kombinace šedé barvy a přírodního dřeva je v moderním designu kuchyní velmi oblíbená. Tento styl působí elegantně, minimalisticky a zároveň přidává prostoru teplý a útulný nádech díky dřevěným prvkům.

![černá.JPG](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_c5c3214c89394b82866522770dc7087e~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_537,h_291,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/%C4%8Dern%C3%A1_JPG.jpg)

## Moderní bílá kuchyň se dřevem – čistota a přírodní elegance

Kombinace bílé barvy a přírodního dřeva patří k nejoblíbenějším trendům v moderním designu kuchyní. Bílá dodává interiéru pocit čistoty, světlosti a vzdušnosti, zatímco dřevěné prvky vnášejí teplo a útulnost. Tento kontrast vytváří harmonický a nadčasový vzhled, který nikdy neomrzí.

![MPIN1523.JPG](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_f02131d288524765a2cd3f66d1b65ae2~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_537,h_291,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/MPIN1523_JPG.jpg)

## Moderní šedá kuchyň v imitaci betonu se dřevem - industriální elegance

Kombinace šedé v imitaci betonu s přírodními dřevěnými prvky je jedním z nejpopulárnějších trendů v moderním interiérovém designu. Tento styl přináší do kuchyně surový industriální vzhled, který je vyvážený teplým, přírodním charakterem dřeva. Výsledkem je elegantní, nadčasový prostor s jedinečnou atmosférou.

![GQIZ6615.JPG](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_2dd8ea71e15c4c5dbdced829f78868fc~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_537,h_291,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/GQIZ6615_JPG.jpg)

## Moderní šedá kuchyň v imitaci betonu s bílou – minimalistická elegance

Kombinace šedé v imitaci betonu s bílými prvky vytváří nadčasový, čistý a elegantní design s lehkým industriálním nádechem. Beton dodává prostoru surový, moderní vzhled, zatímco bílá barva přidává svěžest, lehkost a opticky zvětšuje prostor. Tento kontrast je ideální pro minimalistické i moderní interiéry.

![SREM4313.JPG](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_041561ef84834061a175b329eca6e15a~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_537,h_291,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/SREM4313_JPG.jpg)

# Luxusní keramické obklady a dlažba

Hledáte luxusní keramické obklady a dlažbu přímo od výrobce? Existuje několik špičkových značek, které nabízejí prvotřídní design, vysokou kvalitu a odolnost. Nově vám díky spolupráci s Tiles Studio Jičín umíme vyhovět :).  
  
Tiles Studio Jičín je profesionální koupelnové studio, kde se specializují na prodej kvalitních obkladů a dlažeb pro široké spektrum projektů – od soukromých koupelen po velké komerční zakázky.

![vecteezy_ai-generated-white-marble-bench-surrounded-by-palm-trees_41403069.jpeg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/8dd9cc_f119ed25778a4c1c9b014b8aedac69b0~mv2.jpeg/v1/fill/w_100,h_65,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/8dd9cc_f119ed25778a4c1c9b014b8aedac69b0~mv2.jpeg)

Pokud hledáte individuální přístup při výběru luxusních keramických obkladů a dlažby, Tiles Studio Jičín nabízí kompletní servis.

​

✅ 3D návrhy – možnost vidět, jak budou obklady vypadat v prostoru ještě před realizací.  
✅ Odborné poradenství – pomoc s výběrem správného materiálu, barev, povrchových úprav a technických parametrů.  
✅ Profesionální zaměření prostoru – přesné měření pro eliminaci chyb a optimalizaci řešení.  
✅ Výběr – spolupráce přímo s výrobci, díky čemuž vám můžeme nabídnout špičkovou kvalitu za skvělé ceny

​

Pokud při objednávce použijete kód "PITRIK", čeká na vás speciální nabídka! Jaké výhody přesně získáte? To vám rádi prozradí v Tiles Studio Jičín. 

Neváhejte je kontaktovat a zjistěte, jak si můžete zpříjemnit výběr luxusních keramických obkladů a dlažby. 🎨✨

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/a3c153_bbf1019446e34069a3b96c18f172e810~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_73,h_40,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/a3c153_bbf1019446e34069a3b96c18f172e810~mv2.jpg)

ČASTÉ DOTAZY

Často kladené otázky

Standardní rekonstrukce koupelny trvá přibližně 2-3 týdny v závislosti na rozsahu prací. Menší opravy zvládneme i rychleji.

Ano, na veškeré naše práce poskytujeme záruku. Standardní záruční doba je 24 měsíců.

Ano, v případě potřeby jsme schopni práce naplánovat i na víkendy, abychom neomezili provoz vašeho zařízení.

Cenová nabídka je zdarma. Základě zaměření nebo zaslaných podkladů vám připravíme nezávaznou cenovou kalkulaci do 48 hodin.
